articles.title: THE HIDDEN COST OF HYPERLIQUID — AND HOW TO SKIP IT
articles.summary: You've done the research. You've got your thesis. But before you even place a trade on Hyperliquid, you've already lost money — slippage on the swap, bridge fees, gas on multiple chains.

articles.title: ETHEREUM FOUNDATION RESEARCHERS WARN OF STORAGE BURDEN
articles.summary: Ethereum's state only grows. It is getting harder and more expensive for nodes to store, serve, and sync. Centralization is a risk if only large operators can run full infra. There are three potential initial directions: State Expiry (prune inactive state with proof-based revival), State Archive (separate hot vs. cold state so node performance stays stable), and Partial Statelessness (nodes hold subsets while clients/wallets cache what they need).

articles.title: BITCOIN'S 'REALIZED CAP' HOLDS AT RECORD HIGH OVER $1 TRILLION
articles.summary: Bitcoin's realized cap hit a record $1.125 trillion and continued rising through the 36% correction from October's all-time high, valuing each bitcoin at its last-moved price to show actual capital inflows rather than speculative market cap. The metric's resilience contrasts with 2022's bear market, when realized cap fell from $470 billion to $385 billion during capitulation, with a similar pause during April 2025's tariff tantrum preceding new highs. Bitcoin materially underprices the supportive macro backdrop of resilient global growth, ongoing rate cuts, and steepening yield curves that weaken the dollar, casting doubt on the four-year cycle narrative with potential 2026 upside.

articles.title: SOFI UNVEILS ETHEREUM STABLECOIN FOR TRADING AND PAYMENTS
articles.summary: SoFi Bank's SoFiUSD is a stablecoin debuting on Ethereum positioned as “bank-grade” and aimed at both SoFi's crypto trading flows, payments, and remittances. SoFi hopes to share yield with holders and partners and expand to multiple chains over time, plus offer white-labeled stablecoin infrastructure for other companies.

articles.title: COINBASE ADVISOR: AI FINANCIAL ADVISOR
articles.summary: Coinbase Advisor is an AI-powered financial advisor designed to manage your financial life. It turns natural-language requests into clear, actionable financial plans using the products, data, and tools available on Coinbase. It also surfaces “Daily Insights,” including news stories and proactive recommendations. Users can ask for recommendations using a chat interface and get an in-chat executable widget for Coinbase financial primitives like USDC Lend and prediction markets. It is in beta, with a larger release coming soon.

articles.title: WHO OWNS 'AAVE': AAVE LABS VS AAVE DAO
articles.summary: Aave Labs replaced Paraswap with CowSwap on the Aave website, redirecting 15-25 bps swap fees from DAO treasury to Labs-controlled addresses, leading to an estimated $10 million in annual DAO losses as the protocol-versus-product boundary collapsed. Aave Labs may have a fiduciary duty since the Aave brand was economically paid for through DAO governance, risk absorption, and treasury spending over years, while Labs claims the frontend operation sits outside protocol governance and monetization is justified. The tension escalated with Horizon generating $100k revenue versus $500k DAO incentives plus GHO opportunity costs, Labs bypassing ACI's MegaETH proposal to secure 30 million points directly, and ERC-4626 Aave Vaults positioning Labs-owned products as default Aave V4 UX, capturing fees built on DAO reputation.

articles.title: COINBASE SUES STATES OVER PREDICTION MARKETS
articles.summary: Coinbase filed preemptive lawsuits against Michigan, Illinois, and Connecticut regulators, challenging their authority to classify prediction markets as unlicensed gambling, arguing they're CFTC-regulated financial derivatives protected by federal preemption over state gambling statutes, mirroring Kalshi's successful legal framework. The cases will determine whether platforms face fragmented state-by-state licensing regimes or nationwide federal oversight, enabling Coinbase's "universal exchange" strategy expanding beyond spot crypto into derivatives, tokenized assets, and prediction markets.

articles.title: COINBASE SHOULD BE MOVED TO THE TOP OF NEO BANK LIST
articles.summary: Coinbase now looks more like a top “neobank” because its latest rollout adds agent-to-agent (A2A) payments and an AI financial advisor, which could materially improve revenue-per-employee and operating leverage versus TradFi. It also claims Coinbase's x402 positioning + deep issuer partnerships (e.g., Circle planning for “billions of agents” over 3–5 years) could put Coinbase/Base in the path of trillions of agentic transactions.

articles.title: JPMORGAN'S TOKENIZED DOLLARS ARE REWIRING HOW WALL STREET MOVES MONEY
articles.summary: JPMorgan moved its JPM Coin deposit token (“JPMD”) from its permissioned Ethereum setup on Kinexys onto Coinbase's Base, saying institutions want a bank-deposit “cash equivalent” on public chains where the main option today is stablecoins. The near-term use case is collateral plus margin payments tied to crypto trading (e.g., clients posting collateral at Coinbase), with JPM emphasizing permissioning/whitelisting and strong controls (it controls the smart contract and keys).

articles.title: STABLECOINS BECAME CORE FINANCIAL INFRASTRUCTURE IN 2025
articles.summary: BVNK reached $30B in annualized stablecoin payment volume across 2.8M transactions.

articles.title: PENDLE REVENUE
articles.summary: Pendle is a “blue-chip yield” protocol with $33M revenue (cumulative, YTD 2025), ranking #3 in lending and money-market revenue behind Aave and Spark.

articles.title: INTUIT TO INTEGRATE USDC STABLECOIN ACROSS TURBOTAX, QUICKBOOKS
articles.summary: Intuit and Circle have entered into an agreement for USDC adoption use across TurboTax and QuickBooks.

articles.title: LIDO OUTLINES $60M PLAN TO EXPAND BEYOND LIQUID STAKING
articles.summary: Lido, a leading Ethereum liquid staking protocol, is expanding into vaults and other onchain products to optimize user holdings.
